R Notebook

This is an R Markdown Notebook. When you execute code within the notebook, the results appear beneath the code.

Try executing this chunk by clicking the Run button within the chunk or by placing your cursor inside it and pressing Ctrl+Shift+Enter.

library(readr)
## Warning: package 'readr' was built under R version 4.5.2
library(tidyverse)
## ── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ──
## ✔ dplyr     1.1.4     ✔ purrr     1.1.0
## ✔ forcats   1.0.0     ✔ stringr   1.5.2
## ✔ ggplot2   3.5.2     ✔ tibble    3.3.0
## ✔ lubridate 1.9.4     ✔ tidyr     1.3.1
## ── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ──
## ✖ dplyr::filter() masks stats::filter()
## ✖ dplyr::lag()    masks stats::lag()
## ℹ Use the conflicted package (<http://conflicted.r-lib.org/>) to force all conflicts to become errors
a <- read_delim("https://github.com/jkim2252666/ai_strategy/raw/main/marketing_campaign.csv",
                delim = "\t", 
                escape_double = FALSE,
                trim_ws = TRUE)
## Rows: 2240 Columns: 29
## ── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## Delimiter: "\t"
## chr  (3): Education, Marital_Status, Dt_Customer
## dbl (26): ID, Year_Birth, Income, Kidhome, Teenhome, Recency, MntWines, MntF...
## 
## ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
## ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.
a <- a %>%
  mutate(
    TotalSpent = MntWines + MntFruits + MntMeatProducts + 
      MntFishProducts + MntSweetProducts + MntGoldProds,
    child = Kidhome+Teenhome,
    age = 2025 - Year_Birth)
# outlier
a<-a[-2234,]
# Causal ML
# install.packages("grf")
library(grf)
## Warning: package 'grf' was built under R version 4.5.2
library(ggplot2)
# 조건 변수(Covariates): Income, Age, Child 등 효과를 조절할 후보들
# 결과(Outcome): log(TotalSpent)
# 처치(Treatment): Response (0 또는 1)
X <- as.matrix(a[, c("Income", "age", "child")]) 
Y <- log(a$TotalSpent)
W <- a$Response
# Causal Forest 모델 학습
# 이 모델은 Income, Age, Child의 복합적인 상호작용을 자동으로 파악
cf <- causal_forest(X, Y, W, num.trees = 2000)
# 개별 고객별 CATE(개인화된 효과) 추정
# 모든 데이터 포인트에 대해 '이 사람에게 캠페인이 미칠 영향'을 계산합니다.
cate_estimates <- predict(cf, X)$predictions
a$cate <- cate_estimates
# 결과 확인: 변수 중요도 (어떤 변수가 효과 차이를 가장 많이 만드는가?)
var_imp <- variable_importance(cf)
rownames(var_imp) <- colnames(X)
print(var_imp)
##             [,1]
## Income 0.5734463
## age    0.1758699
## child  0.2506838
# 의사결정 나무를 통해 효과가 높은 집단의 특징 추출
# CATE를 종속변수로 하여 어떤 조건에서 효과가 극대화되는지 시각화
library(rpart)
library(rpart.plot)
## Warning: package 'rpart.plot' was built under R version 4.5.2
tree_model <- rpart(cate ~ Income + age + child, data = a)
rpart.plot(tree_model, main = "CATE 기반 타겟 고객 세분화")

# 자녀 수에 따라 소득별 마케팅 효과(CATE)가 어떻게 다른 지 확인 (그림 3.43 참조)
ggplot(a, aes(x = Income, y = cate, color = as.factor(child))) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.2, se = TRUE) +         # 개별 데이터 포인트 (투명도 조절)
  geom_smooth(method = "loess") +   # 비선형 추세선 추가
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "소득과 자녀수 조건별 CATE",
    x = "소득",
    y = "CATE",
    color = "자녀수"
  )
## Warning in geom_point(alpha = 0.2, se = TRUE): Ignoring unknown parameters:
## `se`
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
## Warning: Removed 24 rows containing non-finite outside the scale range
## (`stat_smooth()`).
## Warning: Removed 24 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range
## (`geom_point()`).

# 나이를 해석하기 쉽게 3개 구간으로 범주화
a <- a %>%
  mutate(AgeGroup = cut(age, 
                        breaks = c(0, 35, 55, Inf), 
                        labels = c("Young (under 35)", "Middle (35-55)", "Senior (55+)")))

# 다차원 시각화 실행
ggplot(a, aes(x = Income, y = cate, color = as.factor(child))) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.2) +          # 개별 고객 데이터 (투명도 조절)
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = TRUE) +    # 그룹별 비선형 추세선
  facet_wrap(~ AgeGroup) +                      # 나이대별로 그래프 분할
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set1") +
  labs(
    title = "소득, 자녀수, 나이 조건별 CATE",
    x = "소득",
    y = "CATE",
    color = "자녀수"
  ) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom", strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"))
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
## Warning: Removed 24 rows containing non-finite outside the scale range
## (`stat_smooth()`).
## Removed 24 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range
## (`geom_point()`).

When you save the notebook, an HTML file containing the code and output will be saved alongside it (click the Preview button or press Ctrl+Shift+K to preview the HTML file).

The preview shows you a rendered HTML copy of the contents of the editor. Consequently, unlike Knit, Preview does not run any R code chunks. Instead, the output of the chunk when it was last run in the editor is displayed.
